bibliographic.citation: Eugen Kölbing, 'Zwei mittelenglische Bearbeitungen der Sage von St. Patrik's Purgatorium,' Englische Studien 1 (1877), 57–121.
bibliographic.title: Zwei mittelenglische Bearbeitungen der Sage von St. Patrik's Purgatorium

entities.description: Kölbing's 65-page Englische Studien article, combining a historical and source introduction with editions of two Middle English versions, German notes, and an October 1876 editorial dateline.
entities.label: Eugen Kölbing, Zwei mittelenglische Bearbeitungen der Sage von St. Patrik's Purgatorium (1877)

physicalDescription.extent: printed pp. 57–121 within Englische Studien volume 1
notes: The article begins below the preceding article on p. 57 and ends above the next article on p. 121; the encoded selection begins lower on p. 98 and ends with Kölbing's signature on p. 121.

rights.basis: The article was published in 1877 and is outside United States copyright term; Internet Archive metadata also records no visible copyright notice in the scanned volume.
rights.component: Kölbing's 1877 article text, edited transcriptions, notes, and typographic presentation

access.entityLabel: National Library of Scotland, Advocates MS 19.2.1 (Auchinleck manuscript)

access.accessMethod: Contact Archives and Manuscript Collections; the exact catalog says physical access is restricted and a digital surrogate is available.
access.entityLabel: National Library of Scotland, Advocates MS 19.2.1 (Auchinleck manuscript)

entities.description: The principal and incomplete manuscript witness used for Kölbing's Version I; the work now cataloged as St Patrick's Purgatory and the Knight, Sir Owen occupies ff. 25r–31v, or columns ff. 25ra–31vb in the project transcription.

holding.notes: The current codex contains i–viii + 334 + ix–xiv folios plus modern paper endleaves; detached fragments survive in other repositories.

entities.label: National Library of Scotland, Advocates MS 19.2.1 (Auchinleck manuscript)

physicalDescription.extent: i–viii + 334 + ix–xiv folios, with 14 stubs and modern paper endleaves; detached fragments survive elsewhere
notes: Probably produced in London; many leaves and miniatures are lost. Version I lacks its beginning and manuscript title.

rights.basis: The anonymous fourteenth-century text and witness predate modern copyright terms by centuries.
rights.component: Anonymous medieval textual content represented by the Auchinleck witness
rights.jurisdiction: United States and jurisdictions with ordinary author-term copyright limits
notes: This does not cover modern transcriptions, photographs, descriptions, interfaces, or repository services.

access.entityLabel: British Library, Cotton MS Caligula A II

rights.basis: The service separates image ordering from reuse and licensing, and no item-level open license was found.

entities.description: A two-part paper manuscript containing the Version II text Owayne Miles at current ff. 91v–95r in Part 1. Kölbing instead cites legacy f. 89 ff.; the exact foliation shift is not inferred.
entities.label: British Library, Cotton MS Caligula A II

physicalDescription.extent: 210 folios in two parts, with early modern endleaves ff. 1–2 and 140–141
notes: Current binding is a British Museum binding dated 1957. The target work lies between The Fifteen Signs before Doomsday ending on f. 91r and Visio Tundalie beginning on f. 95v.

rights.basis: The anonymous fifteenth-century text and witness predate modern copyright terms by centuries.
rights.component: Anonymous medieval Owayne Miles textual content

entities.description: A collateral later-fifteenth-century copy of the Version II tradition: Sir Owen the Knight, IMEV 1767, occupies ff. 28r–38r and is followed by a blank f. 38v.

holding.notes: Restricted material; consultation requires permission of the appropriate curator.

entities.label: Beinecke Library, MS 365 (Book of Brome)

physicalDescription.extent: modern ff. 1–81: 78 leaves plus flyleaves; 12 leaves and one flyleaf missing, with no cataloged loss in the literary texts
notes: The target text was written by Scribe 1 on ff. 28–38. The main texts are associated with East Anglia near the end of the fifteenth century; that witness context is not transferred to composition.
physicalDescription.support: paper with an original late-fifteenth-century vellum wrapper

rights.basis: Beinecke states that supplying reproductions does not authorize publication and that requests are subject to review; no item-level open image license was found.

dateStatements.label: catalog range
dateStatements.notes: The broad range covers the guardbook's heterogeneous fragments and is not a date for Owayne Miles alone.

entities.description: A guardbook of manuscript and printed leaves with commentary by William E. Moss. Part 1 contains collateral Owayne Miles binding fragments mounted as fols. 15, 18, 22, 25, 27, and 28.
entities.label: Bodleian Libraries, MS. Eng. poet. d. 208

notes: The Middle English leaves were recovered from a binding made by Garret Godfrey of Cambridge (active 1503–1539) and mounted by William E. Moss.
physicalDescription.support: manuscript and printed leaves mounted in a guardbook

rights.basis: Special-collections copying and imaging are mediated case-by-case services; no item-level open license was found for the guardbook.

next_steps: Obtain or inspect complete Cotton MS Caligula A II images and a repository foliation concordance; retain current ff. 91v–95r as the authoritative discovery locator meanwhile.
openQuestions.question: How does Kölbing's legacy Cotton locus f. 89 ff. crosswalk to the current British Library locator ff. 91v–95r?

next_steps: Request all provider-exposed physical components through the digitisation service—binding boards/covers, spine, pastedowns, endleaves, blanks, and all 210 folios in both parts—while mapping target ff. 91v–95r separately and asking for image count, cost, restrictions, and reuse terms.
openQuestions.question: Can the British Library supply a complete, reusable image set for Cotton MS Caligula A II?

next_steps: Request the entire physical object—original vellum wrapper, sewing, exposed turn-ins/pastedowns, flyleaves, blanks, and all leaves—with target ff. 28r–38r mapped separately; obtain image-count, completeness, cost, and publication terms before reuse.
openQuestions.question: Will the Beinecke curator authorize consultation or complete-object photography of restricted MS 365, and on what reuse terms?

next_steps: Request binding/boards/covers, spine, pastedowns, endleaves, blanks, guards, and all mounted leaves, with target fols. 15, 18, 22, 25, 27, and 28 mapped separately; ask for exact sequence, overlap with the Auchinleck text, image count, cost, and reuse terms.
openQuestions.question: Can Bodleian Libraries supply a complete physical-guardbook image set for MS. Eng. poet. d. 208 and clarify the Owayne Miles fragment sequence and reuse terms?

next_steps: Review modern source studies and critical editions beyond landing-page abstracts; preserve Kölbing's cautious fuller-common-source hypothesis and do not assert direct derivation between the two versions without stronger evidence.
openQuestions.question: What exact intermediary and stemmatic relationships connect the two Middle English versions to each other and to the Latin Tractatus tradition?

next_steps: Ask U-M Digital Content and Collections for selection, keying, proofreading, and revision notes while retaining the directly observed source mapping.
openQuestions.question: What item-level U-M production record explains selection of printed pp. 98–121 and the 2016 removal of opening prose fragments?

lineage.summary: CME00066 is the repository XML for a University of Michigan manually keyed and proofread electronic text derived from selected matter in Eugen Kölbing's 1877 Englische Studien article. The encoding starts within printed p. 98 at the first of two distinct anonymous Middle English verse versions and ends with Kölbing's signature on p. 121; it omits the article introduction on pp. 57–98 but retains Version I from the Auchinleck manuscript, German commentary, Version II from Cotton MS Caligula A II, and a final German typographic note. A complete 560-page Internet Archive PDF of Englische Studien volume 1 and its metadata/scandata sidecars are cached, as are the complete PDF, OCR, metadata, and scandata for volume 7 containing Kölbing's 1884 re-collation corrections to Version I. The NLS Auchinleck project's complete verified 701-image public inventory is cached with target ff. 25r–31v mapped separately. Current official catalogs identify collateral copies in Beinecke MS 365 and Bodleian MS. Eng. poet. d. 208; no complete public image objects were found for Cotton, Beinecke, or the Bodleian guardbook, so mediated whole-object reproduction routes are recorded without treating partial or absent imagery as complete.

catalogSummary.region: England; the precise places of composition are unresolved, while the surviving witnesses belong to distinct London and later medieval English production contexts
catalogSummary.title: Zwei mittelenglische Bearbeitungen der Sage von St. Patrik's Purgatorium

catalogingSubject.label: Zwei mittelenglische Bearbeitungen der Sage von St. Patrik's Purgatorium
catalogingSubject.scope_note: The edited composite represented by University of Michigan CME item CME00066: Eugen Kölbing's 1877 presentation of two distinct anonymous Middle English verse versions of the St Patrick's Purgatory narrative, separated by his German textual commentary and followed by a German typographic note and editorial dateline. This record does not collapse the two medieval versions, their manuscript witnesses, the Latin source tradition, Kölbing's complete article, the U-M encoding, or the repository XML into one homogeneous medieval work.

contentParts.label: I. [Purgatorium Sancti Patricii]
notes: The XML has 1,188 L elements because the first encoded stanza includes two dotted placeholder lines for lost text; the current NLS project heading reports 1,186 lines in Easting's lineation.
contentParts.summary: Anonymous Middle English tail-rhyme version from the Auchinleck manuscript, lacking its beginning and manuscript title.

contentParts.label: Anmerkungen
contentParts.summary: Kölbing's German textual and comparative notes following Version I.

contentParts.label: II. Owayne Myles
notes: Kölbing cites legacy f. 89 ff.; the current British Library catalog places Owayne Miles at ff. 91v–95r. The foliation crosswalk remains unresolved.
contentParts.summary: Anonymous Middle English couplet version transcribed by Kölbing from Cotton MS Caligula A II.

contentParts.label: Typographic note and editorial dateline
contentParts.summary: German note explaining italic letterforms used to represent manuscript strokes, followed by Breslau, October 1876 and Kölbing's signature.

dateStatements.display: Kölbing dated the edited composite Breslau, October 1876
Kölbing's edited composite was published in 1877
notes: This is the publication date of the cataloging subject, not the composition date of either medieval version.

dateStatements.display: Version I is attested in the Auchinleck manuscript, probably produced c. 1331–1340
notes: The date belongs to the surviving witness and supplies a terminus ante quem, not an exact composition date.

dateStatements.display: Version II is attested in later fifteenth-century copies, including Cotton MS Caligula A II (1450–1499) and Beinecke MS 365 (fourth quarter of the fifteenth century)
notes: The range summarizes witness attestation and is not narrowed into an unsupported composition date.

extent.description: Four encoded content parts: Version I with 198 line groups and 1,188 L elements, 22 German commentary paragraphs, Version II with one line group and 682 L elements, and one final German note paragraph. These are XML representation metrics, not normalized critical line totals.

formStatements.description: Two Middle English verse texts with substantive German prose commentary and a final prose editorial note
Version I is a tail-rhyme poem; Version II is in rhyming couplets

languageStatements.label: Latin
notes: Latin is embedded or editorial rather than the primary language of either poem.
The two Middle English poems are modeled as distinct anonymous adaptations within a modern edited composite; Kölbing's German commentary is not assigned to either medieval author.
Witness production dates and places are not transferred automatically into medieval composition claims.
The XML's 1,188 Version I L elements include two dotted loss markers and therefore differ from the NLS project's 1,186-line heading; both metrics are preserved in their own representation contexts.
The complete NLS project image inventory is a digital-facsimile access object, not evidence that every physical exterior or preservation master is publicly exposed.

next_steps: Obtain or inspect complete Cotton MS Caligula A II images and a repository foliation concordance; retain the current catalog locus as authoritative for discovery meanwhile.
openQuestions.question: How does Kölbing's legacy Cotton locus f. 89 ff. crosswalk to the current British Library locator ff. 91v–95r?

next_steps: Use each repository's mediated reproduction route to request the complete physical object, including binding or wrapper, covers, spine, pastedowns, endleaves, blanks, guards, and all leaves, while identifying the target folios separately and obtaining explicit reuse terms.
openQuestions.question: Can complete, reusable whole-object image sets be obtained for Cotton MS Caligula A II, Beinecke MS 365, and Bodleian MS. Eng. poet. d. 208?

next_steps: Review modern critical editions and source studies beyond their public landing-page abstracts; preserve Kölbing's cautious fuller-common-source hypothesis rather than asserting direct derivation between the two versions.
openQuestions.question: What exact intermediary and stemmatic relationships connect the two Middle English versions to each other and to the Latin Tractatus tradition?

next_steps: Ask U-M Digital Content and Collections for selection and revision notes while preserving the directly observed XML-to-print mapping and the 2016 revision description.
openQuestions.question: What item-level U-M production record explains selection of printed pp. 98–121 and removal of the article introduction?

notes: Breslau is an editorial place, not a medieval place of composition.
The exact intermediary between this Middle English version and the Latin St Patrick's Purgatory tradition remains unresolved.

scope.description: Manuscript readings, notes, and corrections in the 1877 edited presentation

summaries.value: Kölbing presents two anonymous Middle English verse tellings of Sir Owayne's journey through St Patrick's Purgatory. Version I, incomplete at the beginning in the Auchinleck manuscript, is arranged in 198 tail-rhyme stanzas. German notes then discuss readings and the relation between the versions. Version II, transmitted here from Cotton MS Caligula A II, narrates the same otherworld journey in rhyming couplets and ends with the knight's return, later pilgrimage, penitential life, and salvation. A short German note explains typographic signs in the transcription.

The encoding begins within printed p. 98 at Version I and continues through the signature on p. 121. It omits Kölbing's article introduction on pp. 57–98, including the prose above Version I on p. 98, but retains both edited poems, the intervening commentary, the final typographic note, and the Breslau/October 1876 dateline and signature.

titles.value: Zwei mittelenglische Bearbeitungen der Sage von St. Patrik's Purgatorium

notes: Discovery translation of the preferred German title; it is not presented as a historical title.

notes: Kölbing brackets the heading because the manuscript beginning and title are lost.
